14

Marlton Recreation Park

Spacious park....many youth sports leagues are based here. There are pavilions available for rent, walking and jogging trails (which are also paved for bike riding) and three modern playgrounds for the kids to play and have fun.

Di Paolo's Italian Ristorante

This little gem is in the center of Penns Grove, NJ. Family-owned restaurant with fine dining and a wonderful atmosphere. They usually have some great special going on that usually entails tons of delicious authentic Italian food, wine, desserts, etc. This is a restaurant that you must visit. People come from all over to check it out!

Cinder Bar

Nice little spot to watch the game, occasional live music and all around nice vibe. Great food, good drinks, good time.

Valeria Torres

81

La Réserve Paris Hotel & Spa, Paris, France

Description&nbsp;<br><br>La Réserve Paris - Hotel and Spa is a luxurious five-star hotel near the Champs-Elysées, embodying the essence of opulent Parisian living. Opened in 2015, this sanctuary is housed in a mansion formerly owned by Napoleon's step-brother, the Duc of Morny, showcasing 19th-century grandeur harmoniously combined with contemporary conveniences. The interior design, crafted by Jacques Garcia, evokes a bygone era of elegance with its velvet drapery, silk damask walls, gilt frames, and herringbone parquet floors. An intimate garden lounge provides a tranquil retreat from the vibrant city.&nbsp;<br><br>What to Expect&nbsp;<br><br>Guests at La Réserve Paris can anticipate unparalleled privacy and luxury, with 40 rooms that exude elegance through velvet armchairs, antiques, and brocade taffetas, many featuring views of city landmarks. The attention to detail is paramount, with personalized linens, monogrammed bathrobes, and modern amenities like electronic check-in and iPad-controlled room settings. Bathrooms are a testament to luxury, adorned with Carrara and Turquin blue marble and flooded with natural light, enhancing the lavish bathing experience. The service sets a high bar, with dedicated butlers ready to cater to guests' every whim, including arranging bespoke reading material and unique experiences such as private concerts and couture fittings.&nbsp;<br><br>Rooms&nbsp;<br><br>The hotel offers 40 elegantly decorated rooms that are a blend of historical charm and modern luxury. These rooms are furnished with velvet armchairs, antiques, and brocade taffetas, with many rooms offering picturesque views of Parisian landmarks. The rooms are equipped with personalized linens, monogrammed bathrobes, and advanced technological conveniences to ensure a comfortable stay. Restaurants La Réserve Paris boasts distinguished gastronomic venues, including the two-Michelin-star Le Gabriel, which serves French cuisine with a Japanese twist. La Pagode de Cos presents a reinterpretation of global dishes in a setting adorned with carved woodwork and a grand fireplace. For exclusive hotel guests, the Duc de Mornay Library offers a selection of Bordeaux from the owner's vineyards, while Bar Le Gaspard, popular among locals, features an extensive wine and cocktail list.&nbsp;<br><br>Amenities&nbsp;<br><br>Despite its intimate scale, the hotel impresses with an array of recreational facilities. These include a 52-foot lap pool for guests seeking to unwind or maintain their fitness regime, a gym equipped for personal training sessions, and a spa offering anti-aging treatments, providing a comprehensive wellness experience within the heart of Paris.

Brach Paris, Paris, France

Nestled in Paris's sophisticated 16th arrondissement, Brach Paris redefines luxury with a modern twist. This former 1970s postal sorting office, transformed under the creative vision of Philippe Starck, emerges as a multi-level haven of design and casual luxury. With its strategic location, Brach Paris is a stone's throw from iconic landmarks and exudes an air of exclusivity and charm, making it a distinguished choice for those seeking an innovative yet opulent stay in the City of Light.&nbsp;<br><br>What to Expect&nbsp;<br>Guests can anticipate an immersive experience where design meets comfort. The hotel's aesthetic, a blend of high-quality materials such as marble, polished metal, and leather, is complemented by unique touches and contemporary artwork, including pieces by Starck’s daughter, Ara. This attention to detail creates an atmosphere that is both visually stunning and inviting, offering a fresh take on Parisian elegance.<br><br>&nbsp;Rooms&nbsp;<br>The rooms at Brach Paris boast generous proportions and are a showcase of style and sophistication. Decorated with a harmonious mix of rich wood paneling, soft patterned carpets, and serene tones of grays and creams, each room is an enclave of comfort. Artistic elements, from framed sketches to a curated selection of novels and art books, add a personal touch. Advanced in-room technology, opulent marble bathrooms with deep tubs or rain showers, and dedicated sitting areas ensure a stay that is luxurious and rejuvenating.&nbsp;<br><br>Dining&nbsp;<br>Brach Paris presents a culinary landscape that is vibrant and varied. The ground-floor restaurant features an open kitchen and bar, serving a medley of French small plates alongside heartier dishes such as roasted sole and lobster carpaccio. The restaurant's ambiance is matched by a thoughtful wine list and inventive cocktails. Desserts, a highlight of the dining experience, invite guests to select from an array of freshly made patisserie items, enriching the gastronomic journey.&nbsp;<br><br>Amenities & Unique&nbsp;<br>Features Brach Paris stands out for its expansive subterranean sports club, a homage to 1930s boxing gyms, complete with a 23-meter pool, offering a blend of fitness and wellness activities. The spa, adorned with Guerlain products, includes a whirlpool bath, steam room, and sauna, catering to relaxation and rejuvenation. Guests also enjoy exclusive access to a rooftop terrace, offering unique experiences such as sidecar tours of Paris, underscoring the hotel's commitment to providing memorable and bespoke guest experiences. In essence, Brach Paris encapsulates a modern approach to luxury, combining Philippe Starck's design ingenuity with unparalleled amenities and a distinctive culinary experience.

Nolinski Paris, Paris, France

Nolinski Paris, located in the vibrant 1st arrondissement, mere steps from the Palais Garnier opera house and the iconic Louvre, offers an oasis of calm and luxury amidst the city's &nbsp;heart. Since opening its doors, this boutique hotel has distinguished itself through its understated elegance, innovative cuisine, a serene subterranean spa, and rooms that promise a retreat of &nbsp;comfort and style, making it an ideal destination for a Parisian getaway.&nbsp;<br><br>What to Expect&nbsp;<br>From the moment guests enter Nolinski Paris, they are enveloped in a world where Belle Époque Paris meets contemporary sophistication. The hotel, envisioned by renowned architect Jean-Louis Denoit, presents a luxurious abode that could belong to a well-traveled connoisseur, blending marble surfaces with modern art and geometric lines. With its signature scent and carefully selected lobby music, Nolinski creates a sanctuary that shields guests from the bustling city, offering a unique blend of tranquility and Parisian flair.&nbsp;<br><br>Rooms<br>The 45 rooms and suites at Nolinski Paris exude a modern opulence, merging classic hospitality with contemporary amenities. Each room is thoughtfully designed, featuring bluetooth-enabled vintage Marshall speakers and bespoke furniture that reflects the hotel's commitment to individuality and luxury. The attention to detail extends to the selection of French and English books, large beds adorned with fine French linens, and bathrooms that combine marble and gold tones with plush rugs and custom toiletries, ensuring every stay is both lavish and comfortable.<br><br>Dining Nolinski&nbsp;<br>Le Restaurant has elevated its culinary offerings from classic brasserie to innovative gastronomy under the guidance of Philip Chronopoulos, previously of Michelin-starred Restaurant du Palais Royal. The menu focuses on high-quality ingredients crafted into memorable dishes, such as the sweet and spicy tuna lemon and jalapeno starter and the flambeed beef fillet. For guests seeking an exceptional dining experience, reservations can be made at the hotel’s sister restaurant, ensuring a gastronomic adventure that is both sophisticated and delightful.&nbsp;<br><br>Amenities & Unique Features&nbsp;<br>Nolinski Paris boasts a wealth of amenities designed to enhance the guest experience. The expansive subterranean spa features a sauna, hammam, and an elegantly lit 16-metre pool, providing a tranquil escape. The hotel's commitment to service is evident in its 24/7 room service, personal training sessions, and eco-conscious practices, such as the elimination of single-use plastics. Additionally, the hotel's strategic location makes it an ideal base for exploring Paris's cultural and artistic landmarks, with most major attractions within walking distance.
